title: Design a Chat System
interviewPrompt: For today's Software Engineer interview, design a chat system like WhatsApp.

beats.interviewer: For today's Software Engineer interview, design a chat system like WhatsApp.
beats.interviewee: Basic path, sending is easy: the client holds a WebSocket to a chat server, and the server routes each message to the recipient's connection.
beats.show: Send = a WebSocket to a chat server, routed to the recipient's socket.

beats.interviewer: So where's the actual hard part?
beats.interviewee: The recipient is offline half the time, and one server can't hold a socket to everyone. So I need to know which server holds their connection, and store the messages they haven't received.
beats.show: Hard part: route across servers, and deliver to people who are offline.

beats.interviewee: Keep a user-to-server map in Redis. On send, look up the recipient's server and forward. If they're offline, write the message to their inbox queue, something like Kafka, and deliver on reconnect.
beats.show: Presence map in Redis routes; offline messages queue in Kafka.

beats.interviewee: Same fan-out choice as a feed. Small groups, fan the message into each member's inbox. Huge groups, store once and pull on read. The real problem here is offline delivery and ordering, not the socket itself.
beats.show: Group = fan-out for small, pull-on-read for huge. Ordering is the real problem.
